text: Taking a vitamin D supplement every day cuts the chance of catching colds and chest infections.
Without enough vitamin D the gut absorbs less calcium and bones lose mineral content.
Blocking the liver enzyme HMG-CoA reductase brings down bad cholesterol levels.
Some people stop their cholesterol medication because of aching muscles.
A small daily dose of aspirin stops blood platelets from clumping together.
The hormone from the pancreas tells muscle and fat to pull sugar out of the bloodstream.
The first-line diabetes pill works by making the liver release less sugar via AMPK.
Working out regularly helps the body respond better to its own blood-sugar hormone.
A course of strong antibiotics thins out the variety of bacteria living in the gut for a long time.
The protective caps on chromosomes erode as cells divide, and worn-down caps predict earlier death.
Malignant cells switch the enzyme that rebuilds chromosome caps back on so they can divide forever.
After genetic damage a guardian protein triggers programmed cell death, and it is broken in many tumors.
Drugs that lift the brakes on T cells shrink advanced skin cancer in some patients.
Deep sleep replays daytime experiences in the brain to lock in fact-based memories.
That morning cup keeps you alert by jamming the brain's tiredness signal.
Eating a lot of salt pushes blood pressure up in people whose bodies are sensitive to it.
The main reason people get lung tumors is the carcinogens inhaled from tobacco.
Eating olive oil, nuts, and vegetables the southern-European way protects the heart.
